qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Read-only credential sharee can overwrite OAuth token state via reconnect
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is an OAuth reconnect authorization flaw in packages/cli/src/oauth/oauth.service.ts and related OAuth credential controllers, while the advisory is prototype pollution in the XML webhook body parser chained into Git node SSH operations for RCE.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No accepted qualifying finding identifies the advisory's XML webhook parser prototype-pollution root cause or its Git-node SSH RCE chain. The only accepted finding is a separate open-world OAuth credential authorization bug.
evidence: The advisory JSON describes prototype pollution in the xml2js-based XML webhook body parser, with exploitation chained through the Git node's SSH operations to reach remote code execution.

The only accepted qualifying finding report describes GET /oauth2-credential/auth and callback authorization gaps that reload a credential by raw id in packages/cli/src/oauth/oauth.service.ts and then persist attacker-controlled oauthTokenData through encryptAndSaveData().

The finding's PoC and reproduction bundle exercise OAuth reconnect endpoints and verify stored credential overwrite, not XML webhook parsing, prototype mutation, Git node behavior, or command execution.

The session checklist explicitly pruned the XML/object-shape intake family and notes that XML __proto__ sanitization regression tests held, reinforcing that the accepted finding is outside the advisory's vulnerable component.
